change_comments.message:
Few Comments.
I still have to review more files.
I have reviewed this patch completely and have added necessary comments

this is a serious piece of code, quality looks pretty good!
But to properly review I think we need to organise a walktrough as I mention below
Thanks!
Partially resolved comments, will resolve other by Monday 😊 Will plan on a code walk through when Lee Anjella is back
Resolved some comments. Will work on integration tests in a separate patchset. Currently I am preparing for a code walk through as mentioned in few comments

some food for thought...
I suggest you put a few things on WIki about the overall algorithm before we continue again
And maybe discuss the Wiki before implementing...

I like the approach introducing a dummy composite query executor for now.
Still a bit of tidy up required though
I resolved the new comments. Should I mark the old comments which are no longer valid as the code has been moved?

inline_comments.message: @Getter can be used on the requiresAllConditions field and this can be removed

This is expectation.
So not needed in label

Change parameter name to anchorEntity.
Same in lines 82, 93, 109, 127, and 135.

Remove Exceptation from label
There are multiple places this has to be done
we can keep baseCpsPath to indicate it holds the first CPS Path of the composite query

for every id a new Hashset is created.
This can be avoided and a single hashset can be used and cleared upon entering the loop everytime.

This is repeated a few times. 
Suggestion to have a method whcih returns boolean for the presence of child conditions
In the current file it is used only inline 127. I did a Ctrl+F to check. Can you let me know the line Where this repeated?

it is there in 154 with not.
But it is only 2 places so can be avoided.

I think I confused this with
fragmentEntity.getChildFragments() != null

This is present in multiple places 118,191 213,244,295

Heading of this test say it will return all fragments intersection and it is returning empty set. This is creating confusion.

Also, the next testcase "AND operator returns empty set when #scenario" also returns empty sets and only conditions are changes , so can these two be combined?

In this test case can other Descendant options be added i.e Direct and Omit

For OMIT , a testcase is there at line 126 but for Direct Children it is mising
Process composite query when base cpsPath matches nothing
This can be added in 'then' label

Not needed.

There are expectations in multiple test labels.
Please remove those.

There are comments over all the test cases below this line

All those test cases are understandable, but some can be rephrased to be more readable

It is understandable but a bit confusing and lengthy.
Can be it rephrased?
since it is in 2 places we can leave it as it is
it does not test any unique branch so one scenario is sufficient
I checked some of those tests were redundant, so I have removed some of them and kept the ones needed
removed this test due to redundancy

since this support more advance queries why are you copying the old name+V2
why not something like 
getNodesByDataspaceAndAnchorAndCompositeQuery

To be honest I am not a fan of the 'bySomething' naming pattern when something is already a parameter ie part of the signature. I think it was a hibernate pattern that somebody started using every in the code :-( 
To me it is duplicate information, leads to unnecessary long method names and is hard to maintain properly as signature evolves...

that said it can be difficult to have unique methos names if you have many simalar methods my tactic would be to start with a basic name fro teh most basic version and then extend it with just one, max two word to distinigh it from teh mehto befoer it e.g.


getNodes
getNodesV2
getNodesPaginated
getNodesCompositeQuery

rename to sampleDataNodeAsMap and add
expectedDataNodeAsJson to be reused wherever you check the response for this

since this is a 'delegation' test keep the data to the minimum needed (as long as it passes validation:
I woudl prefer '{myQuery}' but because of validation you might need somethign like '{"cpsPath":"myPath"}'

I dont see the point of this test. You mock the empty reponse and then verify the response is empty too. There is no special code path in the controller for empty responses.. This test adds no value, only maintenance cost...
shoudl be same as class name
add javaDoc so I can understabn what this method does, noe I have no idea, especialyl what the return is...
method name is singular, but return type is plural?!
do the user can have multiple conditiosn and if there is no operator you assume AND?!
If you use UpperCase instead can can drop the name property and automatically convert a String to Enum without iterating

at first glance it looks pretty good. But this code is complex and is hard to review properly without any prior knowledge.
I think I stop here with in depth review, for somethign as complex like this we often call a team-walktrough review session. I suggest we do that here to, start with 1 hoor session, invite the whole team start with 1h. Somebody ELSE then the author should share screen and record the comments in gerrit, it might need more then one 1 hour session!
I might glance over the remainder and give some naming tips etc.
But my ecommen
our normal patetrn woudl be baseFragmentEntities but given the context and the length of tehnames I think fragments is ok

it a real good test class, you build it up nicely but I still need some clarification especialy regarding the collection of Longs in the later test case!
AS before, a walktrough would be useful here
NB: do complete (all!) where bloks with something like 'the following case-variations of operators operators are used'
I think an integration test might be more appropriate (and easier) here
NB: do start checking your copyrights

data nodes from query service are returned
(since you are mocking the serive it important to describe it like that)

I copied old name + Payload + V2 to be consistent with current naming convention. But I do agree this convention can sometimes lead to lengthy names. I have renamed to getNodesCompositeQuery as it fits the use case here. 

For future we can update the CPS Developers Guide with a simpler naming convention.
the return type is also singular, i.e. the Operator after it is resolved

yes user can have multiple conditions, and the default operator is set to AND.

Conditions are defined using cps path like /books[@price>14], there can be multiple such cps path conditions combined.

Operators hold AND/OR values.

so I am doing logical operations on fragment entities and using their fragment id as identifier. the fragment id's data type is Long represented as 1L, 2L in groovy. I replaced it with named variables like FRAGMENT_ID_1, FRAGMENT_ID_2, etc
yes , mayeb we can add it to the code review checklist
let's discuss during the walkhtrough I still see an 's' at the end of CompositeQueryOperators
tha'ts a good improvement, much clearer now. Thanks

extract out business logic into a spearet class
e.g. CompositeQueryEvaluator

dont think this is needed. even wth descendant on if there is nothing it will return nothing quickly...
Goin to DB twice now for noe good reason
do this check on baseFragmentsWithDescendants  instead

flatFragmentEntitiesPerId

Consider leaving it as a tree and use a tree-walker instead to avoid re-building tree later...
I dont think we need to get all the data for the node first and then intersect with the first query, just execute the first query , then intersect the next (for AND) or create Union for OR
do not at parameters in methods
NB: theer might be a better/more obvious way of create an imutable copy..
Done and moved the business logic to service layer as having this in ri layer is programmatically incorrect.
removed the 's' as it was confusing and incorrect
marking resolved as this is no more present in new algorithm
this is removed and only one call is being made in service layer code
removed the logic of flattening and now we are using a walking tree approach
NB: no longer needed because you move the logic to different layer I assume

Probably easier and better to write a simple placeholder test that asserts what is does now (thrown an exception I think)
There is a risk this gets forgotten in the next patch and we could miss slippage in future coverage
SonarQube might complain about this being unused for now, maybe better to remvove it until Part 2...
is this all really needed (ie is it validated) or can it be reduced to minimum valid json like {"my":"query"}
the (same) datanodes from the query service are returned
i prefer hard coding these values(yes 3 times)  adn use 'my-....' as you are asserting them
yes I have removed it, looks like you reviewed patch 24 but latest is patch 33 where this is removed 😊
Done. But I noticed we never had java doc for data models, like DataNode and DeltaReport.
It can be used; this was left unhandled from my research phase where it required manual injection

that is correct IMHO i having JavaDoc is superflous for well written code but we agreed in the past to always add it for pubic APIs (incl models)
I totaly agree we haven't applied it conistently (yet)
NB: rename to andOperator for readability
Sorry I missed this but I will add it in the second patch for this API
